speaker: DIOMEDES
text: What, are you up here, ho? Speak.

speaker: CALCHAS
text: Who calls?

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Diomed. Calchas, I think? Where’s your

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: daughter?

speaker: CALCHAS
text: She comes to you.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Stand where the torch may not discover us.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Cressid comes forth to him.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: How now, my charge?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Now, my sweet guardian. Hark, a word with you.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Yea, so familiar?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: She will sing any man at

speaker: ULYSSES
text: first sight.

speaker: THERSITES
text: And any man may sing her, if he

speaker: THERSITES
text: can take her clef. She’s noted.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Will you remember?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Remember? Yes.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Nay, but do, then, and let your mind be

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: coupled with your words.

speaker: TROILUS
text: What should she remember?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: List!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Sweet honey Greek, tempt me no more to folly.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Roguery!

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Nay, then—

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I’ll tell you what—

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Foh, foh, come, tell a pin! You are forsworn.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: In faith, I cannot. What would you have me do?

speaker: THERSITES
text: A juggling trick: to be secretly open!

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: What did you swear you would bestow on me?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I prithee, do not hold me to mine oath.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Bid me do anything but that, sweet Greek.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Good night.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hold, patience!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: How now, Trojan?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Diomed—

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: No, no, good night. I’ll be your fool no more.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Thy better must.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Hark, a word in your ear.

speaker: TROILUS
text: O plague and madness!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: You are moved, prince. Let us depart, I pray you,

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Lest your displeasure should enlarge itself

speaker: ULYSSES
text: To wrathful terms. This place is dangerous;

speaker: ULYSSES
text: The time right deadly. I beseech you, go.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Behold, I pray you.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Nay, good my lord, go off.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: You flow to great distraction. Come, my lord.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I prithee, stay.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: You have not patience. Come.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I pray you, stay. By hell and all hell’s torments,

speaker: TROILUS
text: I will not speak a word.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: And so good night.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Nay, but you part in anger.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Doth that grieve thee? O withered truth!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: How now, my lord?

speaker: TROILUS
text: By Jove, I will be patient.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Guardian! Why, Greek!

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Foh foh! Adieu. You palter.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: In faith, I do not. Come hither once again.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: You shake, my lord, at something. Will you go?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: You will break out.

speaker: TROILUS
text: She strokes his cheek!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Come, come.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Nay, stay. By Jove, I will not speak a word.

speaker: TROILUS
text: There is between my will and all offenses

speaker: TROILUS
text: A guard of patience. Stay a little while.

speaker: THERSITES
text: How the devil Luxury, with his fat

speaker: THERSITES
text: rump and potato finger, tickles these together.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Fry, lechery, fry!

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: But will you, then?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: In faith, I will, la. Never trust me else.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Give me some token for the surety of it.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I’ll fetch you one.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: You have sworn patience.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Fear me not, my lord.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I will not be myself nor have cognition

speaker: TROILUS
text: Of what I feel. I am all patience.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Now the pledge, now, now, now!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Here, Diomed. Keep this sleeve.

speaker: TROILUS
text: O beauty, where is thy faith?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: My lord—

speaker: TROILUS
text: I will be patient; outwardly I will.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: You look upon that sleeve? Behold it well.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: He loved me—O false wench!—Give ’t me again.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Whose was ’t?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: It is no matter, now I ha ’t again.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I will not meet with you tomorrow night.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I prithee, Diomed, visit me no more.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Now she sharpens. Well said,

speaker: THERSITES
text: whetstone.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: I shall have it.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: What, this?

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Ay, that.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: O all you gods!—O pretty, pretty pledge!

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Thy master now lies thinking on his bed

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Of thee and me, and sighs, and takes my glove,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And gives memorial dainty kisses to it

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: As I kiss thee.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Nay, do not snatch it from me.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: He that takes that doth take my heart withal.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: I had your heart before. This follows it.

speaker: TROILUS
text: I did swear patience.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: You shall not have it, Diomed, faith, you shall not.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I’ll give you something else.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: I will have this. Whose was it?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: It is no matter.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Come, tell me whose it was.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: ’Twas one’s that loved me better than you will.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: But now you have it, take it.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Whose was it?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: By all Diana’s waiting-women yond,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: And by herself, I will not tell you whose.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Tomorrow will I wear it on my helm

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: And grieve his spirit that dares not challenge it.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Wert thou the devil and wor’st it on thy horn,

speaker: TROILUS
text: It should be challenged.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Well, well, ’tis done, ’tis past. And yet it is not.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: I will not keep my word.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Why, then, farewell.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Thou never shalt mock Diomed again.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: You shall not go. One cannot speak a word

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: But it straight starts you.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: I do not like this fooling.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Nor I, by Pluto! But that that likes not you

speaker: TROILUS
text: Pleases me best.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: What, shall I come? The hour?

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Ay, come.—O Jove!—Do, come.—I shall be plagued.

speaker: DIOMEDES
text: Farewell, till then.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Good night. I prithee, come.—

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Troilus, farewell. One eye yet looks on thee,

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: But with my heart the other eye doth see.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Ah, poor our sex! This fault in us I find:

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: The error of our eye directs our mind.

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: What error leads must err. O, then conclude:

speaker: CRESSIDA
text: Minds swayed by eyes are full of turpitude.

speaker: THERSITES
text: A proof of strength she could not publish more,

speaker: THERSITES
text: Unless she said “My mind is now turned whore.”

speaker: ULYSSES
text: All’s done, my lord.

speaker: TROILUS
text: It is.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Why stay we then?

speaker: TROILUS
text: To make a recordation to my soul

speaker: TROILUS
text: Of every syllable that here was spoke.

speaker: TROILUS
text: But if I tell how these two did co-act,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Shall I not lie in publishing a truth?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Sith yet there is a credence in my heart,

speaker: TROILUS
text: An esperance so obstinately strong.

speaker: TROILUS
text: That doth invert th’ attest of eyes and ears,

speaker: TROILUS
text: As if those organs had deceptious functions,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Created only to calumniate.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Was Cressid here?

speaker: ULYSSES
text: I cannot conjure, Trojan.

speaker: TROILUS
text: She was not, sure.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Most sure she was.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Why, my negation hath no taste of madness.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Nor mine, my lord. Cressid was here but now.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Let it not be believed for womanhood!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Think, we had mothers. Do not give advantage

speaker: TROILUS
text: To stubborn critics, apt, without a theme

speaker: TROILUS
text: For depravation, to square the general sex

speaker: TROILUS
text: By Cressid’s rule. Rather, think this not Cressid.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: What hath she done, prince, that can soil our mothers?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Nothing at all, unless that this were she.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Will he swagger himself out on ’s

speaker: THERSITES
text: own eyes?

speaker: TROILUS
text: This she? No, this is Diomed’s Cressida.

speaker: TROILUS
text: If beauty have a soul, this is not she;

speaker: TROILUS
text: If souls guide vows, if vows be sanctimonies,

speaker: TROILUS
text: If sanctimony be the gods’ delight,

speaker: TROILUS
text: If there be rule in unity itself,

speaker: TROILUS
text: This is not she. O madness of discourse,

speaker: TROILUS
text: That cause sets up with and against itself!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Bifold authority, where reason can revolt

speaker: TROILUS
text: Without perdition, and loss assume all reason

speaker: TROILUS
text: Without revolt. This is and is not Cressid.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Within my soul there doth conduce a fight

speaker: TROILUS
text: Of this strange nature, that a thing inseparate

speaker: TROILUS
text: Divides more wider than the sky and Earth,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And yet the spacious breadth of this division

speaker: TROILUS
text: Admits no orifex for a point as subtle

speaker: TROILUS
text: As Ariachne’s broken woof to enter.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Instance, O instance, strong as Pluto’s gates,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Cressid is mine, tied with the bonds of heaven;

speaker: TROILUS
text: Instance, O instance, strong as heaven itself,

speaker: TROILUS
text: The bonds of heaven are slipped, dissolved, and loosed,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And with another knot, five-finger-tied,

speaker: TROILUS
text: The fractions of her faith, orts of her love,

speaker: TROILUS
text: The fragments, scraps, the bits and greasy relics

speaker: TROILUS
text: Of her o’er-eaten faith are given to Diomed.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: May worthy Troilus be half attached

speaker: ULYSSES
text: With that which here his passion doth express?

speaker: TROILUS
text: Ay, Greek, and that shall be divulgèd well

speaker: TROILUS
text: In characters as red as Mars his heart

speaker: TROILUS
text: Inflamed with Venus. Never did young man fancy

speaker: TROILUS
text: With so eternal and so fixed a soul.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Hark, Greek: as much as I do Cressid love,

speaker: TROILUS
text: So much by weight hate I her Diomed.

speaker: TROILUS
text: That sleeve is mine that he’ll bear on his helm.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Were it a casque composed by Vulcan’s skill,

speaker: TROILUS
text: My sword should bite it. Not the dreadful spout

speaker: TROILUS
text: Which shipmen do the hurricano call,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Constringed in mass by the almighty sun,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Shall dizzy with more clamor Neptune’s ear

speaker: TROILUS
text: In his descent than shall my prompted sword

speaker: TROILUS
text: Falling on Diomed.

speaker: THERSITES
text: He’ll tickle it for his concupy.

speaker: TROILUS
text: O Cressid! O false Cressid! False, false, false!

speaker: TROILUS
text: Let all untruths stand by thy stainèd name,

speaker: TROILUS
text: And they’ll seem glorious.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: O, contain yourself.

speaker: ULYSSES
text: Your passion draws ears hither.

speaker: AENEAS
text: I have been seeking you this hour, my lord.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Hector, by this, is arming him in Troy.

speaker: AENEAS
text: Ajax, your guard, stays to conduct you home.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Have with you, prince.—My courteous lord, adieu.—

speaker: TROILUS
text: Farewell, revolted fair!—And, Diomed,

speaker: TROILUS
text: Stand fast, and wear a castle on thy head!

speaker: ULYSSES
text: I’ll bring you to the gates.

speaker: TROILUS
text: Accept distracted thanks.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Would I could meet that rogue Diomed! I

speaker: THERSITES
text: would croak like a raven; I would bode, I would

speaker: THERSITES
text: bode. Patroclus will give me anything for the intelligence

speaker: THERSITES
text: of this whore. The parrot will not do more

speaker: THERSITES
text: for an almond than he for a commodious drab.

speaker: THERSITES
text: Lechery, lechery, still wars and lechery! Nothing

speaker: THERSITES
text: else holds fashion. A burning devil take them!
